explainability.path_to_improvement: Document how explanations are provided to users affected by AI decisions.

external_accountability.path_to_improvement: Obtain external validation of AI practices or require vendor certifications.

fairness.path_to_improvement: Document bias testing procedures or vendor AI fairness requirements.

governance.findings: A press release mentions the use of "AI-powered" capabilities and "Artificial Intelligence" to improve forecasting and product availability, supporting governance. The press release explicitly references "AI Governance & Trust" and indicates commitments to responsible AI practices.
governance.path_to_improvement: Name an AI governance body with defined mandate covering all AI use.

relevant_sources.summary: This press release provides evidence for responsible AI by mentioning the use of "AI-powered" capabilities and "Artificial Intelligence" to improve forecasting and product availability, supporting transparency and governance. Furthermore, the press release explicitly references "AI Governance & Trust" and a "Privacy Policy," directly indicating commitments to responsible AI practices and data privacy.
relevant_sources.title: Leading US convenience chain Casey's selects RELEX to optimize space plans and supply chain

oversight.path_to_improvement: Document human review processes for AI-assisted decisions (built or vendor AI).

privacy.findings: A press release explicitly references a "Privacy Policy". This document indicates commitments to data privacy practices.
privacy.path_to_improvement: Publish AI-specific privacy impact assessments or data handling procedures.

relevant_sources.summary: This press release provides evidence for responsible AI by mentioning the use of "AI-powered" capabilities and "Artificial Intelligence" to improve forecasting and product availability, supporting transparency and governance. Furthermore, the press release explicitly references "AI Governance & Trust" and a "Privacy Policy," directly indicating commitments to responsible AI practices and data privacy.

transparency.findings: Casey's General Stores utilizes "AI-powered" capabilities and "Artificial Intelligence" for forecasting and product availability, with sources referencing "AI Governance & Trust". A generative AI named "Darn-ell" is described as promoting coffee by offering personalized and witty comments to customers. This description outlines the AI's function and its customer-facing application.
transparency.path_to_improvement: Document AI systems in use, including vendor/third-party AI tools.

relevant_sources.summary: This press release provides evidence for responsible AI by mentioning the use of "AI-powered" capabilities and "Artificial Intelligence" to improve forecasting and product availability, supporting transparency and governance. Furthermore, the press release explicitly references "AI Governance & Trust" and a "Privacy Policy," directly indicating commitments to responsible AI practices and data privacy.
relevant_sources.title: Leading US convenience chain Casey's selects RELEX to optimize space plans and supply chain

relevant_sources.summary: This third-party report excerpt provides evidence for the **transparency** pillar of responsible AI. It describes Casey's General Stores' use of a generative AI named "Darn-ell" to promote coffee, highlighting its purpose of offering personalized and witty comments to customers about their coffee selection. This narrative description of the AI's function and its customer-facing application supports transparency by informing about the existence and intended use of the AI system.
relevant_sources.title: How Convenience Stores Are Leveraging Artificial Intelligence

summary.overall_findings: Casey's General Stores' published materials document the use of AI-powered capabilities to improve forecasting and product availability, supporting governance practices. Additionally, a press release explicitly references a "Privacy Policy" and commitments to data privacy, bringing the total to 2 of 7 evaluated responsible AI pillars with documented evidence. Drawing from 12 publicly available sources, no qualifying public evidence was found for the majority of the 7 evaluated pillars.
